semanticCore: Satisfying, contenting, giving wealth to store/keep, acquiring permanent assets, or high nose bridge

keyDerivations.meaning: To satisfy, content, give wealth to keep/store, or make rich permanently

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَأَنَّهُ هُوَ أَغْنَىٰ وَأَقْنَىٰ (53:48): And that it is He who enriches (gives temporary wealth) and contents/satisfies (or gives permanent assets to keep).
discardedNoise: Qana (spear shaft) - related spelling but secondary.
summaryEn: The root signifies contenting or providing permanent, stored assets (Qany), praising God who provides both immediate livelihood (Ighna) and long-term security/satisfaction (Iqna).
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الرضا والقناعة واكتساب الأصول المدخرة وإعطاء ما يقتنى ويلازم العبد (قني)، مقرراً أن الله وحده هو مغني الفقراء ومرضيهم بالرزق والرضا.
rawText: قنى Short: (K:) an epithet applied to a man. (TA.)
